---
<<<<<<< HEAD
title: "Nobel prize data"
output: flexdashboard::flex_dashboard
=======
title: "The Nobel Prize"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme: flatly
>>>>>>> 20799537ec7ccaca658bcaa49cec4aee4dab1259
runtime: shiny
---


```{r global, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)

library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
```

Intro
==========================

<<<<<<< HEAD
Column {data-width = 200}
-------------------------

**Background Information**

For our final project, we are analyzing data found on the official Nobel Prize website. Information we grabbed with the Nobel Prize API includes information about the Nobel Prize Laureates like : age, gender, birth date, birth country, prize amount, prize category, and their names. With this project, our goal is to identify historical patterns as well as potential inequalities in Nobel Prize awards by answering key questions. 

-------------------------

**Data sources **

we accessed our data from the Nobel Prize website

- [Website](https://www.nobelprize.org/) 

- [Page with the API](https://www.nobelprize.org/about/developer-zone-2/)

- [API](https://api.nobelprize.org/2.1/laureates)

-------------------------

**Link to Github**
 
[code] ()
=======
Column {data-width=800}
-------------------------


#### Background Information

The Nobel Prize is one of the most prestigious international awards, recognizing individuals and organizations that have made major contributions to science, literature, economics, and peace. 

In this Shiny dashboard, we analyze Nobel Prize data collected from the official Nobel Prize API. The dataset includes information about laureates such as age, gender, birth country, prize category, prize amount, and year awarded.

Using this project, we explore historical trends in Nobel Prize awards across different time periods and categories. We also investigate patterns related to gender representation and geographical distribution in order to better understand inequalities and changes in recognition over time.



#### Data Sources

- [Nobel Prize Website](https://www.nobelprize.org/)
- [Nobel Prize API Page](https://www.nobelprize.org/about/developer-zone-2/)
- [Nobel Prize API](https://api.nobelprize.org/2.1/laureates)

#### GitHub

Here's a link to our [code](https://github.com/)

Column
-------------------------

```{r, out.width="60%"}
knitr::include_graphics("nobel_medal.webp.png")
```
>>>>>>> 20799537ec7ccaca658bcaa49cec4aee4dab1259
 
Patterns
==========================

Inputs {.sidebar}
-------------------------


```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

Nobel <- read_csv("Nobel_final_Data.csv")

ggplot(Nobel, aes(x = Prize_year)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Nobel Laureates Over Time",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Count")
```

Column 
-------------------------

### Nobel Prizes by Gender & Period

```{r echo = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

nobel <- Nobel |>
  mutate(Cent = case_when(
    Prize_year >= 1901 & Prize_year <= 1925 ~ "1901-1925",
    Prize_year >= 1926 & Prize_year <= 1950 ~ "1926-1950",
    Prize_year >= 1951 & Prize_year <= 1975 ~ "1951-1975",
    Prize_year >= 1976 & Prize_year <= 2000 ~ "1976-2000",
    Prize_year >= 2001 & Prize_year <= 2025 ~ "2001-2025"
  )) |>
  drop_na(Gender)

ggplot(nobel, aes(x = Cent, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Nobel Prizes by Gender & Period", x = "time frame", y = "proportion")
```

Men have received the most awards throughout the years. More than 80% percents of the laureates are males. The number of women Nobel Prize winners has increased in the past half century but they are still evidently low.  

-------------------------

### Nobel Prizes by category & Period

```{r echo = FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

nobel <- nobel |>
  mutate(Category = fct_rev(fct_infreq(Category)))

ggplot(nobel, aes(x = Cent, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Nobel Prizes by Gender & Period", x = "time frame", y = "proportion") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d()
```

New emerging categories such as economic sciences, literature is declining 

Tidyverse
==========================

Inputs {.sidebar}
-------------------------

```{r, echo=FALSE}
inputPanel(
  selectInput("n_breaks2", label = "Number of bins:",
              choices = c(5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30), selected = 15),
  
  sliderInput("bw_adjust2", label = "Bandwidth adjustment:",
              min = 0.1, max = 3, value = 1.2, step = 0.1)
)
```

Column 
-------------------------

### Age distribution

```{r, echo=FALSE}
renderPlot({
  ggplot(nobel, aes(x = Age)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), 
                   bins = as.numeric(input$n_breaks2),
                   fill = "white", colour = "black") +
    geom_density(adjust = input$bw_adjust2, colour = "blue") +
    labs(x = "Age", title = "Age distribution")
})
```
